content.problemStatement:
Great! You have successfully built a **Student Database Manager** using Java.

Here are some improvements you can try:

- **Use a Database** – Instead of using text files, switch to a structured database like **SQLite**, **MySQL**, or **PostgreSQL** for better scalability and data integrity.
- **Auto-load Records** – When the program starts, automatically load and display all existing student records to give users a quick overview.
- **Advanced Search** – Enhance the search feature to support **partial matches**, or filter by **department** and **course**.
- **Graphical User Interface (GUI)** – Implement a user-friendly interface using **JavaFX** or **Swing** to improve usability and visual appeal.

### **Final Updates:**
- Ensure all features — **Add, View, Search, Update, Delete** — work as expected.
- Validate user input (e.g., correct age range, valid name format).
- Handle edge cases gracefully and display informative error messages.
- Provide clean and consistent user prompts throughout the app.

---

By making these enhancements, you’ll take your **Java Student Database Manager** to the next level! 🚀
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
